betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Oil Derrick§f

§aDrill down to oil deposits and pump crude oil to the surface — the cheapest entry point into petroleum!§f

The Oil Derrick is a machine that extracts crude oil and natural gas from underground oil deposits. When supplied with power, it drills downward until it reaches a deposit and continuously extracts oil and gas until the deposit is depleted.

§6Operation§f:
Place the derrick directly above a confirmed oil deposit and supply power. It will automatically drill down and begin cycling.
§b• Power Consumption§f: §e2kHE/s§f during operation.
§b• Extraction Rate§f: §e500mB§f crude oil + §e100-500mB§f natural gas per §e2.5 second§f cycle.
§b• Oil Buffer§f: §e64,000mB§f internal crude oil storage.
§b• Gas Buffer§f: §e64,000mB§f internal natural gas storage.
§b• Depletion Risk§f: §c5%§f chance per cycle to deplete the deposit block.
§b• IO§f: Crude oil pushes into containers via the topmost slot. Natural gas uses a separate IO slot.
§b• Upgrades§f: Accepts machine upgrades in dedicated slots.

§cWarning§f: If the derrick drills through §bUranium Ore§f or §bAsbestos Ore§f, §cdeadly poison gas§f will be released at the surface. Always wear a §bGas Mask§f when working near operating derricks.

§eTip§f: The §bNatural Gas§f byproduct is valuable — store it for later refinement into §bPetroleum Gas§f and §bUnsaturated Hydrocarbons§f via the §bCatalytic Cracking Tower§f, rather than burning it off.
